rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Therma Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed on September 24. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD LANE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Golden Gate Drywall, Inc'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling On Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall Inc.¿s motion for reconsideration of the ruling on its motion for summary judgment is granted. On its own motion and without regard to compliance with CCP 1008, the court reconsidered the denial of defendant¿s motion for summary judgment. Upon reconsideration of defendant¿s motion for summary judgment, the motion is granted. Now that there is no doubt that plaintiffs have been apprised that defendant sought summary judgment on the ground of lack of asbestos content of any products attributable to defendant that Mr. Lane was exposed to and plaintiffs have had the opportunity to provide any evidence they might have on the issue of asbestos content, departure from the so-called ¿golden rule¿ is warranted. Accordingly, based on a further review of the papers filed on the initial motion and on this motion for reconsideration, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence that any of the products described in Mr. Cantley¿s declaration attributable to defendant that Mr. Lane was exposed contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RILEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to any asbestos-containing insulation attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA JOAN HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Wheelabrator Technologies Inc.¿s motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is off calendar because plaintiffs previously dismissed their request for punitive damages and represent that it remains dismissed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA JOAN HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Wheelabrator Technologies Inc.¿s demurrers to the first, second, fifth and sixth causes of action in plaintiff¿s second amended complaint are denied. Plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint sufficiently alleges the asbestos-containing products on which plaintiffs seek to impose liability against defendant. Further specificity may be sought through discovery. Defendant¿s demurrers to the third and fourth causes of action are off calendar because plaintiffs previously dismissed those causes of action and represent that they remain dismissed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEULAH MAE SIGSBEY et al VS. A.O. SMITH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Carrier Corporation's motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens. The motions to dismiss for forum non conveniens (fnc) and all joinders: thereto are denied and all alternate motions to stay and joinders thereto are granted. This action is stayed pending a determination whether Michigan has jurisdiction over all defendants. While defendants did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in Michigan, the fnc factors show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum and that, if it can take jurisdiction over all defendants, Michigan is a far more convenient forum. Plaintiffs¿ argument based on defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor¿s asserted lack of amenability to jurisdiction in Michigan fails since the sole evidence cited for that argument is defendant¿s 1998 GO 129 interrogatory responses and those responses show that defendant did work on Navy ships beginning no earlier than 1946 yet plaintiffs¿ complaint alleges that Mr. Sigsbey ¿s last exposure to asbestos resulting from his presence on a Naval ship was in 1946. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. DEFENDANT SB DECKING, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens. The motions to dismiss for forum non conveniens (fnc) and all joinders: thereto are denied and all alternate motions to stay and joinders thereto are granted. This action is stayed pending a determination whether Michigan has jurisdiction over all defendants. While defendants did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction in Michigan, the fnc factors show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum and that, if it can take jurisdiction over all defendants, Michigan is a far more convenient forum. Plaintiffs¿ argument based on defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor¿s asserted lack of amenability to jurisdiction in Michigan fails since the sole evidence cited for that argument is defendant¿s 1998 GO 129 interrogatory responses and those responses show that defendant did work on Navy ships beginning no earlier than 1946 yet plaintiffs¿ complaint alleges that Mr. Sigsbey ¿s last exposure to asbestos resulting from his presence on a Naval ship was in 1946. = (220/HEK)
